architecture.summary: MASPRM is a process reward model that scores intermediate multi-agent system (MAS) states and agent actions conditioned on full routed transcript prefixes, while agents generate actions from their local partial views. The system is trained via MAS-MCTS rollouts using terminal outcome rewards backed up to local action targets without manual step-level annotations. At inference, MASPRM guides search via step-level beam search (SBS) and Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) using Bradley-Terry preference ranking to score successor states, focusing computation on promising branches and pruning unpromising ones.
trained_components: MASPRM value head (1.5B or 7B base model)

biblio.title: MASPRM: Multi-Agent System Process Reward Model

claims.statement: Averaged across GSM8K, MATH, MMLU, and LogiQA, MASPRM improves Hit@1 over policy likelihood by up to +13.4 points

claims.dataset: GSM8K, MATH, MMLU, LogiQA (averaged under MCTS(10,3))
claims.statement: MASPRM improves ranking quality, reducing Hit@1→Hit@5 gaps by up to 10.3 points
MASPRM-guided MCTS(10,3) achieves Hit@1 of 82.9 on GSM8K vs 62.5 for policy likelihood under matched agent calls
Cross-domain MASPRM (trained on GSM8K, tested on MATH) degrades by 5.4 points vs in-domain but improves by +5.4 points vs policy likelihood baseline
MASPRM-7B with MCTS(10,3) is competitive with Mistral 24B-Instruct on GSM8K (82.9 vs 80.4) and outperforms GPT-4o-mini (82.9 vs 62.0)

MASPRM is trained from MAS-MCTS rollouts labeled only with terminal outcome rewards, without requiring human step-level annotations, by propagating returns to local targets via sibling preference pairs
Bradley-Terry preference ranking head outperforms MSE regression by +3.2 to +4.3 points on math benchmarks for the 1.5B model and by +1.2 to +9.6 points for the 7B model under MCTS(10,3)

Training requires verifiable terminal outcomes; extending to imperfect verifiers is noted as an important direction. Study is limited to scheduled MAS with outcome supervision; generality to open-ended interactive environments or real-world deployments is not claimed.
